text: Last class I sent a friendly reminder, and I was still waiting for an answer.
Now the supplier replied, and I want to make an appointment to meet.

Are you free on Wednesday morning?
Would Thursday at three be convenient for you?
I am afraid we have to reschedule our call.
Could we move it to Thursday instead?
Hi Karen, the supplier wants to make an appointment. He has a slot on Wednesday morning. Are you free then?
Oh, there is a problem. Wednesday morning clashes with the board meeting. I am afraid we have to reschedule.
No problem. Let us not cancel -- let us just postpone it. Could we move it to Thursday at three instead?
Thursday at three is convenient for me. Write to him politely. Say we are sorry for the change, and ask if that day works.
I will write: I am afraid we have to reschedule. Could we move it to Thursday at three? Would that day be convenient for you?
That is clear and kind. You did not cancel, you just moved it, and you asked politely. Send the new invite. Great work, Percival.
I would like to make an appointment with you.
Could we move it to Thursday at three instead?
Would that day be convenient for you?

Hi Percival, this is Mr. Santos from the supplier. I would like to make an appointment to review the contract with you. Are you free midweek? I can suggest a time slot on Wednesday morning, around ten o'clock. Would that be convenient for you? If Wednesday does not suit you, I am flexible. We can arrange another day. Please let me know what works, and I will confirm it. I really want to fit this into your schedule, so just tell me your best time. Thank you very much, and I look forward to our appointment. Have a good day.

Hi Percival, this is Karen. Small problem with our appointment. The board meeting now clashes with our call on Wednesday, so I am afraid we have to reschedule. Could you write back to the supplier and politely ask to move it? Do not cancel, just postpone. Suggest Thursday at three instead, and ask if that day is convenient for him. Keep it kind and short. Say we are sorry for the change, and that we want to find a time that suits everyone. If Thursday does not work, ask him to propose another slot. Thank you so much, and let me know once it is arranged.

First, the supplier asks Percival to make an appointment and suggests a slot on Wednesday morning. Then, a board meeting clashes with that time. After that, Percival writes back and politely asks to reschedule. Later, he suggests Thursday at three and asks if it is convenient. Finally, the supplier agrees, and the new appointment is arranged.
